div1.entryFree:
Concealment, 1) forbearance of disclosure, secrecy: let c., like a worm i' the bud, feed on her damask cheek, Tw II, 4, 114. imprison't not in ignorant c. Wint. I, 2, 397. 'twere a c. worse than a theft, to hide your doings, Cor. I, 9, 21. some dear cause will in c. wrap me up awhile, Lr. IV, 3, 54 (== hinder me from disclosing who I am).
2) secret: profited in strange --s, H4A III, 1, 167.
